title: To Get the Strait Open, Trump Had to Leave the Hardest Issues for Later
summary: President Trump is hailing the agreement with Iran as groundbreaking, even as he admits it “isn’t even fully negotiated.” But the nuclear stockpile, enrichment and missiles have not been discussed.

title: Reopening of Strait of Hormuz Still a Question Mark
summary: Without the details of a formal agreement, it is unclear how soon normal shipping will resume and when oil prices will start to come down.

title: Senate Republicans Cast Doubt on a Potential Peace Deal With Iran
summary: Lawmakers, including some of President Trump’s closest allies, slammed the emerging agreement as effectively undermining the president’s own war goals.

title: How Prediction Markets and Crypto Firms Steamrolled a Watchdog Agency
summary: The Commodity Futures Trading Commission purged its ranks, dialed back its enforcement and boosted industries in which President Trump’s family is heavily invested.

title: What to Know About the Citizenship Lists Trump Wants to Create
summary: President Trump is trying to create individual lists of citizens by state to determine who can vote, even as his administration acknowledges they would be unreliable.

title: Confusion and Worry After Trump Administration’s Abrupt Green Card Changes
summary: Immigrants and their advocates and lawyers are trying to interpret a new Trump administration rule that requires people to be in their native country to apply for a green card.

title: Caught Flat-Footed, a City Races to Catch Up With Ebola
summary: The deadly virus has spread alarmingly in Congo for months. Only now is the response taking shape.

title: What to Know About the Ebola Outbreak as Cases and Deaths Rise
summary: More than 170 deaths and about 750 cases are suspected. The United States restricted entry for people who have been to three African countries.

title: They Started I.V.F., Then Split. Now Who Gets Custody of the Embryos?
summary: For 47-year-old Erin Millender, this will likely be her last chance to become a mother. Her husband no longer wants to have a child with her.

title: Bystander Wounded in White House Shooting Is in Stable Condition
summary: The Metropolitan Police Department is investigating to determine who shot the bystander, who underwent surgery, and how many bullets were fired.

title: What to Know About the White House Shooting on Memorial Day Weekend
summary: Shots were fired near the northwestern corner of the White House complex, while President Trump was inside the building. The Secret Service returned fire, killing the gunman.

title: Trump Endorsement Shakes John Cornyn in Texas Senate Runoff Against Ken Paxton
summary: A tough re-election race grew more daunting after President Trump backed Mr. Cornyn’s opponent, Ken Paxton. The Texas senator has vowed to fight to the end.

title: A Democrat Took on Red Sox Ownership in an Ad. A Network Pulled it.
summary: Graham Platner, a Senate candidate in Maine, blamed private equity for destroying “our favorite baseball team.” NESN, which is owned by the team, pulled his campaign ad, citing an intellectual property violation.

title: In Homeless Crisis, California ‘Is Waging a War on R.V.s’
summary: The R.V.s are seen as an eyesore — the most visible sign of the state’s homelessness crisis. Neighbors and politicians want them gone. The people who call them home feel under siege.

title: A Homeowner Built Townhouses in a Jersey City Backyard
summary: As YIMBYs and NIMBYs debate where to build housing, a project in Jersey City, N.J. shows how seemingly irreconcilable sides can find common ground.

title: As Rents Soar, Londoners Turn to Vacant Pubs, Offices and Police Stations
summary: Property guardianship, in which residents live in otherwise vacant buildings, used to be considered edgy. But as the rental market tightens, more people are embracing the arrangement.

title: UK Institute Is Hunting for Dangers Lurking in AI
summary: The government’s A.I. Security Institute, staffed by alumni from OpenAI and Google, is becoming a model for countries grappling with A.I.’s emerging risks.

title: One Job That Is Growing in the A.I. Era? Cybersecurity Experts.
summary: Demand for security engineers has surged as artificial intelligence generates a glut of new code and models like Anthropic’s Mythos create new concerns.

title: A.I. Bots Told Scientists How to Make Biological Weapons
summary: Scientists shared transcripts with The Times in which chatbots described how to assemble deadly pathogens and unleash them in public spaces.

title: Crack Forms in Garden Grove Chemical Tank, a Positive Sign for Firefighters
summary: The tank in Southern California has been building in temperature and pressure for days, which could lead to an explosive release. The crack may reduce some of that pressure, officials said.

title: How a Curious FIFA Deal Gave Fox a Huge Bargain for World Cup Broadcast Rights
summary: A decision to stave off litigation between Fox and FIFA turned into a bonanza worth hundreds of millions of dollars in discounted World Cup rights to the broadcaster.

title: For Carlo Petrini, the Point of ‘Slow Food’ Wasn’t the Food. It Was Us.
summary: The Italian activist, who died on Thursday, built his global movement on the idea that eating well could make modern life more meaningful.

title: Carlo Petrini, Whose Slow Food Movement Transformed How We Eat, Dies at 76
summary: Through his Slow Food organization, he spurred a worldwide shift toward sustainability and thoughtful cuisine.

title: How Democrats Can Win, According to This Nobel Economist
summary: An expert in human behavior explains how Democrats can change their primary process to improve their chances in 2028.

title: As a Doctor, I Can Understand the Allure of ChatGPT
summary: Even doctors can learn something from A.I.

title: In Ukraine, a Divisive 20th-Century Hero Comes Home
summary: Andriy Melnyk, criticized as a Nazi collaborator and lionized as an anti-Soviet resistance leader, was given state honors for his reburial near Kyiv.

title: Israel’s President, Isaac Herzog, Denounces Israeli Violence
summary: In a speech, Isaac Herzog, whose role is largely ceremonial, delivered a grave indictment of settler attacks in the West Bank and abuse of prisoners.

title: Manny Nosowsky, Waggish Crossword Constructor for The New York Times, Dies at 94
summary: For nearly two decades, the prolific puzzle maker, a retired urologist, charmed solvers with his penchant for witty, waggish puns.
